records.ety: [Perhaps of Scand. origin, and originally meaning, to walk on snowshoes; cf. dial. Sw. truga, trudja, a snowshoe, Norw. truga, Icel. þrūga.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Trudged (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Trudging.]

defs: To walk or march with labor; to jog along; to move wearily.
quotes: And trudged to Rome upon my naked feet.
